reasoning: There are no human vaccines for Pasteurellosis available or in late-stage clinical trials. While animal vaccines exist (e.g., for cattle and poultry), the Wikipedia article "List of infectious diseases" correctly listed the vaccine status as "No" as of late October 2025, showing that animal vaccines are not considered for this cell. Therefore, for the resolution to change to "Yes" on any of the target dates, either an unprecedentedly rapid human vaccine development would need to occur (which is practically impossible in this 1–3 month timeframe), or a Wikipedia editor would have to mistakenly add an animal vaccine (or vandalize the page) and the edit would have to survive without being reverted by the resolution date. Given the high visibility of medical articles on Wikipedia, such errors are typically reverted quickly. The probability is set to the minimum allowed value of 0.05 for the first date, slightly increasing for later dates to reflect the minimally growing risk of an uncorrected erroneous edit over time.
